name: Lebanon
summary: Day 56 / Lebanon Ceasefire Day 8 of 10 — DIRECT WASHINGTON TALKS + HEZBOLLAH ROCKETS/DRONES + ISRAELI JOURNALIST KILLED. Lebanese Ambassador to US Nada Hamadeh Moawad meets Israeli Ambassador Yechiel Leiter in Washington for SECOND ROUND of direct talks in three decades — US-brokered mechanism to extend the 10-day truce beyond Sunday April 26 expiry is now explicit diplomacy, not backchannel. Democracy Now April 22 reports Hezbollah fired rockets and drones at Israeli forces FOR THE FIRST TIME SINCE THE TRUCE TOOK EFFECT — ceasefire publicly fraying. Israeli demolition operations continue (Bint Jbeil, Khiam, Markaba, Taybeh); paramedics recovered body of Lebanese journalist hours after Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon Thursday morning. Israeli officials continue stating troops will remain inside southern Lebanon during any truce extension. Structurally, April 26 IST now carries TWO simultaneous expiries: Trump 3-5 day Iran window AND Lebanon 10-day ceasefire — both depend on US mediation success; both have parallel military-readiness escalations. Stacking of two expiries into one weekend materially increases the probability of kinetic escalation in the window.

signals.content: Israel launched ground incursion into southern Lebanon against Hezbollah. Strikes on Beirut.
At least 41 killed in Israeli air attacks on Bekaa Valley. Hezbollah and Israeli troops clashing on ground.
Lebanese PM Salam demanded Hezbollah surrender weapons and restrict to political activities. Emergency Cabinet meeting. ~1,000 dead, 1M+ displaced.

Hezbollah announced 45+ military operations against Israel on March 27, including rockets, drones, and targeting Israeli troops inside Lebanon. Israel expanding ground invasion — sent more troops into southern Lebanon. IDF killed 2 top members of Hezbollah's communications unit in Beirut overnight March 28. Israel claims 800+ Hezbollah operatives killed in Lebanon since March 2. At least 1,189 people killed in Israeli strikes (including 124 children), nearly 1 million displaced (20% of Lebanon's population). Hezbollah chief urged 'unity, no talks with Israel.' Lebanon front remains the most active ground combat theater. (Sources: Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Defense News, Times of Israel)

UNIFIL peacekeeper killed during ongoing Lebanon operations (Al Jazeera March 29). This internationalizes the conflict further — UNIFIL deaths historically trigger UN Security Council responses and European public opinion shifts. Hezbollah maintaining 45+ daily operations. Israel expanding ground invasion, sent more troops into southern Lebanon. IDF killed 2 Hezbollah communications leaders in Beirut. (Sources: Al Jazeera)

Hezbollah 600+ attacks on Israel in 24 hours — doubling prior record. 4 IDF soldiers killed in southern Lebanon combat. 3 Indonesian UN peacekeepers killed in two incidents. France called emergency UNSC session. Iran demands Lebanon be included in any ceasefire — Hezbollah militarily reinforcing that position. CONFIRMED.

Beirut airstrike killed Hezbollah's southern front commander Haj Youssef Ismail Hashem on April 1 — the most senior Hezbollah commander killed since the war began March 2. Seven killed, 26 wounded in the strike. 'Fierce clashes' reported in Shamaa, approximately 5km inside Lebanese territory from the border. France called emergency UNSC session over the Hezbollah escalation. Ground combat continuing despite command-level decapitation strikes. CONFIRMED. (Sources: Al Jazeera)

Israel struck Lebanon April 1 concurrent with Trump primetime address and ongoing Iran campaign. Iran's ceasefire conditions include 'ending all fronts' — Lebanon explicitly named. Hezbollah operational status unchanged. (Sources: NPR, Al Jazeera)

Israeli strikes killed 2 girls and wounded 40 near Tyre. Hospital damaged. IDF soldier killed by friendly fire in southern Lebanon. Bridges destroyed in southern Lebanon — restricting civilian movement and supply routes. (Sources: Al Jazeera, Times of Israel)

Israeli strikes across Lebanon killed at least 15: Beirut Jnah neighbourhood (5 killed, 52 wounded — strike 100m from Rafik Hariri University Hospital, Lebanon's largest public hospital), Ain Saadeh east of Beirut (3 killed, 3 injured), Kfar Hatta (7 killed including a 4-year-old girl). IDF Chief Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir visited troops in southern Lebanon, pledging to 'intensify strikes.' (Sources: Al Jazeera, Manila Times, Al-Monitor)

Hezbollah claimed cruise missile hit on Israeli warship 68 nautical miles off Lebanese coast at midnight — first anti-ship cruise missile claim of the war. Hezbollah said vessel was 'preparing to carry out attacks on Lebanese territory' and 'direct hit confirmed' after hours of surveillance. IDF told AFP: 'We are not aware of it.' CLAIMED/UNVERIFIED — no independent maritime verification. If confirmed, represents major capability escalation (C4ISR sophistication at 68nm range). Masnaa border crossing with Syria FORCED CLOSED after Israel threatened to strike it — civilians now queuing at northern Qaa crossing. Syria's borders director called crossing 'exclusively used by civilians.' Closure evokes Syria 2014 patterns. Total Lebanon war casualties updated: 1,460+ killed (Lebanese Health Ministry), 2,584+ wounded. (Sources: CGTN, Arab News, Xinhua, Times of Israel, Antiwar.com, Manila Times, Al-Monitor)

IDF Northern Command strategic reframe: deems militarily disarming Hezbollah 'unrealistic' (would require occupying all of Lebanon). New objective: 2-3km security zone south of Blue Line. IDF admits 'significant gap' between assessed and actual Hezbollah capabilities after overestimating 2024 ground offensive damage. Haaretz reports IDF 'stretched to breaking point' in Lebanon with 'familiar deadly stalemate.' Four Nahal Brigade soldiers killed recently. Iran's 10-clause ceasefire counter-proposal demands 'regional scope including Lebanon front' — linking Lebanon to broader war resolution. (Sources: Times of Israel, Haaretz, FDD, Axios)

Lebanon casualty total 1,461 killed since March 2 (Lebanese Health Ministry), including at least 124 children; over 1 million displaced. Israel destroying southern Lebanon towns; 'safe areas around Beirut' hit; Christian town of Ain Saadeh hit overnight April 6 (escalation of geographic envelope outside traditional Hezbollah influence area). IDF Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir announced widening of Operation Northern Arrows: 'The damage to Hezbollah will intensify.' Hezbollah participated in the first formally announced tripartite cruise missile/drone salvo with Iran + Houthis April 6. Israeli media not prominently featuring Lebanese casualty numbers — dominant frame is operational success.

EVENING UPDATE: IDF 98th Division deployed, 91st Division active. Lebanon toll risen to 1,497+ killed, 4,639 wounded, 1.2M displaced. IDF reoccupied pre-2024 ceasefire positions. Haaretz: 'not planning to advance deeper' — forward defensive posture. Iran's 10-point counter includes end to Israeli strikes on Hezbollah in Lebanon — linking Lebanon front to broader ceasefire. Masnaa border crossing remains forced closed. (Sources: Times of Israel, Haaretz, Al Jazeera)

LEBANON EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED FROM US-IRAN CEASEFIRE — DEAL'S STRUCTURAL FAULT LINE [CONFIRMED]. Israeli PM Netanyahu, after backing the bilateral US-Iran 2-week pause, explicitly stated the deal 'does not include Lebanon' and reaffirmed continued operations against Hezbollah. Earlier (Saturday April 5), Netanyahu reportedly told senior officials operations against Hezbollah 'would not be halted even if Iran and the United States reached a ceasefire.' This exclusion is the structural risk: Iran's 10-point plan demanded 'end of war across all fronts' including Hezbollah. The bilateral US-Iran framework as structured does not deliver this. IDF struck Christian mountain town Ain Saadeh east of Beirut April 6 killing prominent anti-Hezbollah politician and his wife — operations continue. If Israel strikes Beirut on Day 1 of Friday Islamabad talks, Iran's hardliners can frame the deal as bad-faith.

Operation Eternal Darkness: 50 IAF fighter jets struck 100 Hezbollah targets across Lebanon on April 8 in 10 minutes using ~160 munitions. Central Beirut struck WITHOUT prior warning. At least 254 killed, 1,165 wounded — the single deadliest day of the 2026 Lebanon war. Targets included Hezbollah command centers, intelligence HQ, rocket units, naval units, Radwan Force assets, and aerial unit infrastructure.

Hezbollah initially halted attacks on Israel after ceasefire announcement. After Operation Eternal Darkness (254 killed), Hezbollah resumed missile attacks on northern Israel on April 9 morning. Hezbollah official told AP the group was 'giving a chance for mediators to secure a ceasefire in Lebanon' but 'we have not announced our adherence to the ceasefire since the Israelis are not adhering to it.' Lawmaker Fadlallah called strikes 'a grave violation of the ceasefire' with 'repercussions for the entire agreement.' Hezbollah position: ceasefire INCLUDES Lebanon, directly contradicting Netanyahu and Trump.

Operation Eternal Darkness toll revised UP: 254-300+ killed, 1,150 wounded (Lebanon Health Ministry). Israel struck ADDITIONAL targets April 9 despite ceasefire. Hezbollah RETALIATED with rockets into northern Israel April 9 — first post-ceasefire rocket fire. France (Macron) entered scope dispute: Lebanon 'fully included' in ceasefire — adding fourth voice (Iran/Pakistan/France say included; US/Israel say excluded). Netanyahu authorized direct negotiations with Lebanon at State Department next week (Axios). Vance's offer to Israel to 'restrain Lebanon strikes during talks' appears not honored given April 9 strikes. (Source: PBS, Axios, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel)

Day 44. Nabatieh strike killed 13 Lebanese State Security personnel at government building — the largest attack on that city since the war began. Critically, these are GOVERNMENT security forces, NOT Hezbollah — targeting expanding beyond militia to Lebanese state apparatus. Strikes continued in Aita al-Shaab, al-Majadel (Tyre district), and Hanine. HRW reported Israeli strikes killed hundreds and damaged vital bridge — documenting systematic infrastructure degradation. Hezbollah ESCALATED dramatically: 100+ rockets since ceasefire, 70 on April 10 alone. Hezbollah struck Ashdod Naval Base (~200km from border — qualitative capability escalation to strategic depth). Also struck Haifa, Kiryat Shmona, Karmiel (4-month-old infant and 4 children injured), Nahariya. Trump told Netanyahu to keep operations 'low key' to avoid derailing Pakistan talks — a shift from 'not included' to implicit acknowledgment that Lebanon violence threatens the talks. Ceasefire scope 4-way dispute continues with escalating operational intensity on both sides.

Day 45. Israeli strikes killed at least 18 people across southern Lebanon on April 11: at least 8 in a village near Sidon, at least 10 in Nabatieh district including 3 emergency workers. IDF said it struck 200+ Hezbollah targets in 24 hours. Lebanon PM Nawaf Salam CANCELLED his planned Washington trip, citing need to 'safeguard the security of the Lebanese people and their unity' — this came as Hezbollah supporters rallied in Beirut against any direct negotiations with Israel. Hezbollah lawmaker Hassan Fadlallah rejected direct talks as violating Lebanon's constitutional framework. Ambassador-level talks still set for Tuesday at State Department between Israeli Ambassador Leiter and Lebanese counterpart — but Israel explicitly 'refused to discuss a ceasefire with the Hezbollah terrorist organization.' Iran's Ghalibaf named Lebanon ceasefire as one of Iran's Islamabad preconditions — and talks failed. Total toll since March 2: 2,020 killed, 6,436 wounded (Lebanon Health Ministry).

AFTERNOON UPDATE: Death toll from Nabatieh strike REVISED UPWARD to 13 Lebanese security personnel killed (from earlier 10). These were uniformed internal-security staff, not Hezbollah fighters -- President Joseph Aoun condemned the strike explicitly on this basis. Funerals held Saturday in Sidon. Overnight April 11-12, Israel continued striking southern Lebanon, killing at least 11 more. Two rockets launched from Lebanon toward Israel; sirens activated in Upper Galilee. One drone from Lebanon intercepted. IDF confirmed striking "loaded and ready-to-launch rocket launcher" in Jouaiya. The Lebanon track is the most fragile component of the broader crisis architecture -- Iran explicitly linked Islamabad nuclear talks to Lebanon ceasefire as a precondition. With Islamabad failed and Israel-Lebanon ambassador-level talks set Tuesday at State Dept (downgraded from PM-level), the diplomatic pathway is degrading. (Sources: Lebanon presidency via Business Upturn/ANI, Al Jazeera liveblog, IDF statement, The National)

IDF raided Bint Jbeil hospital, killing ~20 Hezbollah fighters and seizing weapons. IDF cut all main roads to Bint Jbeil with airstrikes, artillery, and white phosphorus. Golani Brigade killed operatives responsible for fallen IDF soldier. Two 98th Division soldiers moderately wounded by Hezbollah April 13. Israel-Lebanon talks still set for Tuesday at State Dept (ambassador level after PM Salam cancellation). Hezbollah FPV suicide drones causing 'significant tactical strain' on IDF armored vehicles. Casualty toll likely above 2,030. (Sources: Times of Israel, JPost, Haaretz, Tribune India)

Israel-Lebanon ambassador-level talks confirmed April 15 at State Department. Fundamental disagreement on agenda persists: Lebanon says about declaring ceasefire, Israel says 'formal peace negotiations' and explicitly refused to discuss ceasefire with Hezbollah. Hezbollah's Naim Qassem explicitly rejected talks as 'futile' and a 'free concession,' urged Lebanon to cancel. Hassan Fadlallah: Hezbollah 'will not abide by any agreements' (CONFIRMED rejection — source: Al Jazeera, Euronews). Hundreds protested in Beirut April 11-12. On ground: IDF near-completion of Bint Jbeil capture, 1.2M displaced. Updated casualties: 2,055 Lebanese killed, 6,588 wounded since March 2 (source: CGTN, Haaretz). Hezbollah's rejection creates structural impossibility — any agreement reached without Hezbollah buy-in is unenforceable on the ground where Hezbollah controls southern Lebanon.

EVENING ELEVATION: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will personally host April 15 Israel-Lebanon talks — elevating from ambassador-level to SecState level, most high-level meeting since 1993 (CONFIRMED — Axios, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, Deccan Herald). Rubio's involvement may reflect US need for diplomatic win after Islamabad failure. However, structural obstacles unchanged: Hezbollah's Naim Qassem explicitly rejected talks as 'futile,' Fadlallah declared Hezbollah 'will not abide by any agreements.' Hundreds protested in Beirut against talks. Iran's Ghalibaf named Lebanon ceasefire as precondition at Islamabad — progress here could unlock broader deal.

CONFIRMED: Ambassador Hamadeh Moawad participated in first direct Israel-Lebanon talks since 1993 at State Department April 14-15. Joint statement: 'agreed to launch direct negotiations' that could 'unlock significant reconstruction assistance and economic recovery for Lebanon.' However: Hezbollah's Naim Qassem called talks 'futile' and a 'free concession,' urged Lebanon to cancel. Hassan Fadlallah: Hezbollah 'will not abide by any agreements.' Hundreds protested in Beirut. Lebanon's government participating diplomatically while its most powerful armed group refuses — the gap IS the structural impossibility. (Sources: US State Department, Axios, Washington Times, Al Jazeera, CBC News, Euronews)

CONFIRMED: Lebanon Health Ministry reported 182 killed in Israeli strikes on Wednesday April 15 — HIGHEST single-day death toll of the Israel-Hezbollah war. Cumulative toll since March 2 rises to ~2,237+ killed. Netanyahu announced Israel is EXPANDING the IDF-occupied area in Lebanon toward parts of the Syrian border. IDF Chief Zamir 'approved further plans' for Lebanon operations. Hezbollah responded with 105 attack waves (Alma Research). Senior US official told Haaretz Trump 'would welcome the end of hostilities in Lebanon as part of a peace agreement between Israel and Lebanon' even if 'this is not something we have asked for nor is it part of the peace negotiations with Iran' — US DE-LINKING Lebanon from the Iran track, contrary to Ghalibaf's Islamabad insistence. Simultaneously Haaretz reported IDF assesses Hezbollah unlikely to accept truce without Iran agreement — contradicts US official's de-linking framing. Lebanese state diplomacy continues independently of Hezbollah, advancing despite pre-emptive rejection. (Sources: Wikipedia 2026 Lebanon war timeline, Times of Israel, Haaretz, Xinhua, Middle East Eye, Al Jazeera)

CEASEFIRE DAY 1 — ASYMMETRIC COMPLIANCE DOCUMENTED [CONFIRMED]. 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire took effect at midnight Beirut time April 16-17. Within hours, Lebanon's army reported Israeli violations: 'intermittent shelling of several southern Lebanese villages,' specifically artillery and machine-gun fire on Al-Khiam and Debbin, and drone attacks in the Western Bekaa region (Al Jazeera, Asia Plus). Army urged residents to delay returning to southern villages. Israel's response was procedural — 'the IDF reserves the right to take all necessary measures in self-defense' (CNN) — effectively pre-authorizing continued strikes under the Netanyahu '10km security zone' framework. Hezbollah confirmed it 'launched its last attack at 11:50pm local time, just 10 minutes before the ceasefire took effect' — clean compliance on its side. Iran FM Baghaei 'welcomed' the ceasefire and said it was 'part of a broader agreement with the US to pause the regional war, mediated by Pakistan,' re-integrating Lebanon into the Iran negotiating track (contradicting US de-linkage). Baghaei demanded Israel 'fully withdraw forces from southern Lebanon,' directly contesting the 10km zone. Lebanese death toll now 2,196 (Lebanon National News Agency). The pause is ASYMMETRIC — Hezbollah honoring it; Israel treating it as a 'timeout' from offensive operations while retaining operational freedom inside the zone. (Sources: Al Jazeera, CNN April 17 liveblog, Asia Plus, Lebanese Army statement, Hezbollah statement, Iran MFA Baghaei)

CEASEFIRE DAY 2 — KOUNINE DEATH + HAARETZ 'NETANYAHU BOXED IN' FRAMING [CONFIRMED]. Israeli drone strike in southern village of Kounine killed one person per Lebanon's Health Ministry (CBC News, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel liveblog April 18). Lebanon NNA reports continued Day 2 violations. IDF characterises strikes as 'self-defense' against Hezbollah operatives. Hezbollah compliance unchanged (politician Ali Fayyad on Al Jazeera Arabic: 'caution and vigilance,' any targeting = breach; no rocket fire). Haaretz April 17 analysis (premium): 'Trump Dictates the Terms in Lebanon and Iran, Leaving Netanyahu Boxed In — Trump hijacked Israeli foreign policy so Netanyahu had no say in Lebanon truce.' This is the sharpest Israeli-establishment critique of Netanyahu's diplomatic position since ceasefire announcement. Haaretz April 5 carry-over: IDF 'admits it can't disarm Hezbollah.' Watch: cabinet instability, Smotrich/Ben-Gvir walkout threats (Day 2 no walkout — hardline bloc accepting pause tactically). Asymmetric compliance pattern established: Hezbollah honors, Israel exercises self-defense framework. Casualty-per-day rate ~1 is LOWEST sustained rate of the war (pre-ceasefire Apr 15 = 182/day). 7 days remain in 10-day window; transition to permanent framework is the next binary. (Sources: CBC News, Lebanon NNA/Lebanese Army via Al Jazeera, Times of Israel liveblog April 18, Haaretz April 17 analysis)

CEASEFIRE DAY 3 — QUIET HOLDS. SINGLE CONFIRMED KINETIC WITHIN WINDOW REMAINS FRIDAY KOUNINE STRIKE [CONFIRMED non-events]. No new IDF strike reports Saturday-Sunday morning IST. The single confirmed kinetic event inside the 10-day cessation-of-hostilities window remains the Friday April 17 IDF drone strike on a motorcycle between Kounine and Beit Yahoun that killed one (Lebanese media, CBC). Hezbollah has launched ZERO rockets into Israeli territory since ceasefire onset (IDF Homefront Command logs per Times of Israel liveblog). State Department April 17 clarification — that Trump's 'Israel PROHIBITED' framing applies ONLY to offensive action and preserves Israel's self-defence lane — defines the operational envelope but has NOT been tested in the quiet window since Friday. Structural fragility unchanged (Hezbollah non-signatory, IDF in-country, non-withdrawal). Day 3 is the QUIETEST interval since pre-March-4 proxy escalation — a remarkable de-escalation baseline if sustained. 6 days remain in 10-day window. (Sources: State Department April 17 release, Times of Israel liveblog April 17 entry with no updates overnight Saturday into Sunday, CBC News, Al Jazeera Lebanon desk)

FIRST IDF FATALITY UNDER 10-DAY CEASEFIRE — KALFON IED + QASSEM 'INSULT' SPEECH + KIRYAT SHMONA PROTESTS [REPORTED multi-source]. WO (res.) Barak Kalfon killed by Hezbollah-attributed IED in Jebbayn during IDF building-search; disclosure Sunday April 19. Three to nine wounded (sourcing inconsistent). Hezbollah SecGen Naim Qassem Sunday speech: ceasefire is 'an insult to Lebanon' but no rocket retaliation order, no kinetic timeline, no axis-of-resistance call. Israeli side: Kiryat Shmona municipal protests + convoy to Jerusalem Sunday = symmetric domestic dissatisfaction. Lebanese government statement not yet surfaced. Ceasefire's structural fragility (Hezbollah non-signatory, IDF in-country, non-withdrawal) is now testing under live conditions: ceasefire HOLDING in aggregate but with first lethal IDF event inside the window.

LEBANON CEASEFIRE DAY 4 — KALFON IED FATALITY DETAILS UNCHANGED; NO IDF RETALIATION; HEZBOLLAH KINETIC RESTRAINT HOLDS [CONFIRMED]. Kalfon (Warrant Officer Barak Kalfon, 226th Reserve Paratroopers Brigade, killed in Jebbayn) remains the only Israeli fatality under the 10-day ceasefire. Defense Minister Katz's Sunday tribute ("brave and dedicated fighter… devoted family man… engineer at Rafael") surfaced with no IDF retaliatory strike. Hezbollah SG Qassem Sunday speech called the ceasefire "an insult to Lebanon" — RHETORIC only, no rockets. Structural reading holds at Day 4: low-signature kinetic permitted + escalatory rhetoric permitted + open rocket war withheld. Ceasefire fragility unchanged (Hezbollah non-signatory, IDF in-country, no withdrawal). April 26 expiry in 6 days.

LEBANON CEASEFIRE DAY 7 OF 10 — HOLDING WITH VIOLATIONS [CONFIRMED]. Lebanese military reports continued Israeli shelling in Khiam, Bint Jbeil, Dibbin. Katz: IDF holds positions. TIO April 20: US reportedly plans ask Lebanon repeal law forbidding contact with Israeli citizens. Washington Round 2 State Dept-hosted Thursday April 23. TIO April 21 Israel 'coordinating attack plans with US' on Iran track — suggests hedging against Iran-track failure while Lebanon deteriorates toward April 26 expiry.

DAY 7 OF 10 — STEADY STATE; ISRAELI DEMOLITION OPERATIONS CONTINUE [CONFIRMED]. Israeli forces continue demolition operations in Bint Jbeil, Khiam, Markaba, Taybeh; Israeli army characterised earlier limited Hezbollah launches as 'a blatant violation of the ceasefire agreement.' Lebanon PM Salam stated Lebanon needs $587 million for humanitarian recovery (Al Jazeera Day 54 piece). Hezbollah kinetic count remains zero rocket launches since ceasefire. Netanyahu said Israel will 'continue striking Iran in coordination with the White House' (separate to Lebanon track) — but no Channel 12/13 leak of imminent IDF Iran-strike preparation Wednesday. Lebanon ceasefire structurally deteriorating as April 26 expiry approaches; kinetic-quiet count holds but ground demolition pattern is the slow-bleed of the ceasefire's ground architecture. Nothing material to update from morning.

DAY 8 OF 10 — DIRECT WASHINGTON TALKS, HEZBOLLAH ROCKETS/DRONES, JOURNALIST KILLED [CONFIRMED]. (a) Lebanese Ambassador to US Nada Hamadeh Moawad meets Israeli Ambassador Yechiel Leiter in Washington for SECOND ROUND of direct talks in three decades — US-brokered mechanism to extend the 10-day truce beyond Sunday April 26 expiry is now explicit diplomacy, not backchannel; (b) Democracy Now April 22: Hezbollah fired rockets and drones at Israeli forces FOR THE FIRST TIME SINCE THE TRUCE TOOK EFFECT — ceasefire publicly fraying; (c) Israeli demolition operations continue, paramedics recovered body of Lebanese journalist hours after Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon Thursday morning; (d) Israeli officials continue stating troops will remain inside southern Lebanon during any truce extension. April 26 IST stacks Trump 3-5 day Iran window expiry + Lebanon 10-day expiry into one weekend — both depend on US mediation; both have parallel military-readiness escalations.
sources: Washington Post April 23 'Lebanon and Israel to resume rare direct talks in Washington'

edges.context: Day 56 / Ceasefire Day 8. Hezbollah fired rockets and drones at Israeli forces FOR THE FIRST TIME SINCE THE TRUCE TOOK EFFECT (Democracy Now April 22) — kinetic-quiet count broken. Ceasefire publicly fraying. Two days remain in window before April 26 expiry; without extension, full re-escalation likely.

Day 56 / Ceasefire Day 8. Israeli demolition operations continue (Bint Jbeil, Khiam, Markaba, Taybeh). Lebanese journalist killed in Israeli airstrike Thursday morning. Lebanese Ambassador Moawad meets Israeli Ambassador Leiter in Washington for second round of direct talks in three decades — US-brokered extension mechanism explicit. Two days remain in window.

Day 56. State Department-hosted Israel-Lebanon Round 2 talks Thursday April 23 in Washington — second round of direct talks in three decades. US-brokered mechanism to extend 10-day truce beyond April 26 expiry. April 26 stacks with Trump Iran 3-5 day window — same US mediation team running both tracks.

trigger_points.condition: Lebanon ceasefire lapses April 26 IST without US-brokered extension OR enters extension framework
trigger_points.mechanism: April 26 stacks with Trump Iran 3-5 day window expiry — same US mediation team running both tracks. Hezbollah rockets/drones Thursday signal ceasefire fraying. Without extension, full re-escalation likely. Compound failure path: Lebanon ceasefire lapses + Trump Iran window expires without proposal → Israel resumes Lebanon ops + activates Iran combat-readiness; Hezbollah responds; oil reload to $103-115. Falsifiable: 23:59 IST Sunday April 26.
